open_known_gaps.summary: viewer.js loadDocument/renderAll has no page-count cap or processing timeout; a high-page-count PDF within the 50MB limit can exhaust client browser memory. Client-side only, no server impact. Fix is a developer task (page-count cap at the viewer.js load boundary). Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

notes: Clean review. New code this tick (present.js full-screen Present feature, app.js wiring) is safe: textContent only, no innerHTML/eval/document.write, no inline handlers. All innerHTML uses across js/ are container clears (= ''), no user content. upload.js validation intact (magic bytes + MIME + .pdf ext + empty + 50MB cap + filename sanitize). Live headers confirm full CSP + 4 security headers; sensitive paths 403/404; .mjs MIME text/javascript; 0 failed SSH auths in 24h; no secrets in web JS. Only open item is SEC-002 (low, client-side PDF-bomb DoS) — tracked for developer in docs/security-guide.md, not security's to implement.
